pattern: Bitmask DP tracking the set of distinct characters in the current partition: try changing each index to each letter and simulate the greedy partitioning, keeping the best result.
hints: Without any change, simulate greedily: extend the current partition while it has at most k distinct chars, starting a new partition when adding the next char would exceed k.
A single character change can only affect partitions that include the changed index — iterate over all n*26 choices, re-simulate, and take the maximum.
Represent the current set of distinct characters as a 26-bit bitmask for O(1) popcount and union.

edge_inputs.description: Single character, k=1 — one partition, no change helps
k=26 — never need more than 1 partition
Change middle character to something new — splits middle partition
k=1 — every distinct character is a new partition

stress.description: Random string of length 100 with k in [1,5]
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase[:8]) for _ in range(size))
    k = rng.randint(1, 5)
    return [s, k]

complexity.time: O(n^2 * 26) for brute force, O(n * 26) optimal with prefix/suffix arrays
